ux_issues.description: The chat session mode field shows CHAT_MODE_COMPANION even though the session was originally created in SELECTION mode. The mode appears to reflect the last message mode rather than the session creation mode, which could be confusing if a user expects sessions to track which mode they started in.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider either tracking the original creation mode separately, or making mode a property of individual messages rather than the session.

ux_issues.description: The first AI response in selection mode was very long — it created itinerary items, handed off to an expert, recommended hotels, and provided driving tips all in a single response. While comprehensive, this wall of text could overwhelm a user on first interaction. A more conversational approach with follow-up questions first might feel more natural.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider having the AI ask a couple of clarifying questions before dumping the full itinerary and hotel recommendations in the first response.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: The AI did not ask clarifying questions about swimming abilities, dietary restrictions, or budget range before making recommendations. The persona test specifically checks whether the AI asks about these before finalizing. Instead, it jumped straight into building a full itinerary and recommending hotels in the very first response.
ai_behavior_issues.context: First message in selection mode. The AI created 8 itinerary items, handed off to an expert who created 3 more, and recommended 3 hotels — all without asking a single clarifying question.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: The AI proactively created itinerary items during selection mode (before the trip even existed in planning mode). While the create_trip tool was called first, the itinerary creation happened in the same response turn, which means the user never had a chance to confirm the trip details before items were added.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI created an excellent family-oriented Costa Rica itinerary with 10 days across 3 locations, realistic driving times, and age-appropriate activities. Context injection worked perfectly — the AI never forgot the destination or family details across all messages. The expert handoff to 'Mateo' (nature/family/adventure specialist) was smooth and the expert provided genuinely useful insider tips. Companion mode responses were concise and practical, especially the urgent medical query which provided specific clinic names and phone numbers. The main weakness was the AI's eagerness — it built the entire itinerary and recommended hotels in the very first message without asking clarifying questions about the family's preferences first. Safety information was specific and practical (clinic names, pharmacy locations, water safety details for non-swimmer Noah), which directly addresses the persona's core concern.
